content.claim: Hungary operates one of the most selective education systems in Europe, with between-school variation in learning outcomes indicating stronger social selection than in Austria, Germany, Lithuania, and the Netherlands, as measured by OECD PISA 2015 science assessment
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: PISA measures social selection through between-school outcome variance, not directly through the 6/8-year track mechanism
Does not isolate the independent causal contribution of the 6/8-year gimnázium versus other selection drivers (free school choice, residential segregation, admissions autonomy)
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
